questions.question_text: Which of these equations is the correct expression for force on a charge in magnetic field?
questions.question_html: Which of these equations is the correct expression for force on a charge in magnetic field?
questions.explanation: A charged particle moving in a magnetic field experiences magnetic Lorentz force given by {tex}\vec F = q\left( {\vec v \times \vec B} \right){/tex}

questions.question_text: Direction of magnetic force on a positive charge moving in a magnetic field is given by
questions.question_html: Direction of magnetic force on a positive charge moving in a magnetic field is given by
questions.explanation: The force experienced by a charged particle in a magnetic field is given by {tex}\vec F{/tex} = q {tex}\left( {\vec v \times \vec B} \right){/tex} .The right hand rule for vector product can be used to determine the direction of {tex}\vec F{/tex}

questions.explanation: The Lorentz Force is given by F = qE + q(v × B). It acts on a charged particle when it moves in the presence of electric as well as magnetic field.

questions.question_text: Correct unit of magnetic field is
questions.explanation: The SI unit of magnetic field is Tesla (T).

questions.question_text: If a current I is flowing in a straight wire parallel to x-axis and magnetic field is there in the y-axis then,
questions.question_html: If a current I is flowing in a straight wire parallel to x-axis and magnetic field is there in the y-axis then,

options.text: the wire experiences force in z-direction
options.html: the wire experiences force in z-direction

options.html: the wire experiences force in x-direction

options.text: the wire experiences force in y-direction
options.html: the wire experiences force in y-direction

questions.correct_option_text: the wire experiences force in z-direction
questions.explanation: Force experienced by a conductor of length {tex}l{/tex} carrying current I placed in magnetic field B is given by {tex}\vec F = I\vec l \times \vec B{/tex} . Now, {tex}I\vec l = \left| {I\vec l} \right|\hat i{\text{ and }}\vec B = \left| {\vec B} \right|\hat j{/tex} Thus, {tex}\vec F = I\vec l \times \vec B = \left| {I\vec l} \right|\hat i \times \left| {\vec B} \right|\hat j {/tex} {tex} = IlB\left( {\hat i \times \hat j} \right) = IlB\left( {\hat k} \right){/tex} The force acts along the positive z-axis.

questions.question_text: Correct expression for force on a current carrying conductor of length dl in a magnetic field is
questions.question_html: Correct expression for force on a current carrying conductor of length dl in a magnetic field is
questions.explanation: Force experienced by a conductor of length dl carrying current I placed in magnetic field B is given by {tex}I(\overrightarrow{dl}\;\times\;\overrightarrow B){/tex} .

questions.question_text: The magnitude of the magnetic field due to a circular coil of radius R carrying a current I at an axial distance x from the centre is
questions.question_html: The magnitude of the magnetic field due to a circular coil of radius R carrying a current I at an axial distance x from the centre is
questions.explanation: Magnetic field at a point located at a distance x from the center of the coil of radius R carrying current along its axis is {tex}B = \frac{{{\mu _0}I{R^2}}}{{2{{({x^2} + {R^2})}^{\frac{3}{2}}}}}{/tex}

questions.question_text: Consider the motion of a charged particle in a uniform magnetic field directed into the paper. If velocity v of the particle is in the plane of the paper the charged particle will
questions.question_html: Consider the motion of a charged particle in a uniform magnetic field directed into the paper. If velocity v of the particle is in the plane of the paper the charged particle will
questions.explanation: The charged particle experiences magnetic Lorentz force given by {tex}\vec F = q\left( {\vec v \times \vec B} \right){/tex} . This force acts in the plane of the paper perpendicular to the velocity vector at all points on the plane. Magnetic Lorentz force acts like a centripetal force, causing the particle to move in a circle.

questions.correct_option_text: accelerate charged particles or ions to high energies
questions.explanation: Cyclotron is a particle accelerator, which uses electric field to accelerate the charged particle and magnetic field to make it move in a circular path. During acceleration in the electric field, the particle acquires high energies of the order of a few million electron volts.

questions.question_text: The magnetic field at a distance r from a long wire carrying current I is {tex}0.4{/tex} tesla. The magnetic field at a distance 2 r is
questions.question_html: The magnetic field at a distance r from a long wire carrying current I is {tex}0.4{/tex}  tesla. The magnetic field at a distance 2 r is
questions.explanation: The magnetic field due to a straight conductor varies inversely as the distance of the observation point from the conductor. Therefore, when the distance is increased from r to 2 r, the magnetic field will become {tex}0\cdot4{/tex} /2 = {tex}0\cdot2{/tex} tesla.
The expression for Ampere’s circuital law is {tex}\oint {\vec B \cdot d\vec l = } {\mu _0}I{/tex} Where I is the current included in the surface bounded by the line integral.
